scope.target: Clarify the canonical repository branch and merge policy without changing current behavior.
constraints: Preserve the canonical path and active references.
Do not introduce classic Git Flow, mandatory hosted PRs, or direct-on-main workflow execution.

steps: Name the model as single-trunk and GitHub-Flow-like with repository-specific rules.
Explicitly exclude classic Git Flow and strict trunk-based interpretation.
Limit mandatory workflow branches to workflow mode.
execution.last_completed_step: Clarified the canonical policy identity, flow classification, classic-Git-Flow exclusion, and workflow-mode-only branch requirement.

results.summary: Retained the canonical path and documented the current single-trunk, short-lived, GitHub-Flow-like model without changing behavior.
results.residual_risk: The historical filename still contains GIT-FLOW for compatibility, while the title and content explicitly reject classic Git Flow.
